title: Miracle Cold Cure
post_text: Doctors hate this: one spoon of silver tonic cures every cold overnight. Share before pharmacies delete this!
red_flags: Miracle cure language; conspiracy framing; no source; medical claim
explanation: Medical claims need reliable clinical evidence and professional guidance. The post uses fear and conspiracy language instead of verifiable evidence.
source_note: Educational simulation based on common health misinformation patterns.

title: Campus Password Reset
post_text: Your student email will close tonight. Confirm your password at bit.ly/verify-scholar to keep access.
red_flags: Urgent deadline; password request; shortened link; unofficial account
explanation: Legitimate IT teams do not ask for passwords through random shortened links. The safest action is to report phishing and verify through official campus channels.
source_note: Educational simulation based on common phishing warning signs.

title: Flood Photo Claim
post_text: This image proves the mayor ignored today's flood damage downtown.
red_flags: No source; image provenance unclear; emotionally charged accusation
explanation: Images can be old, edited, AI-generated, or shown out of context. Verify with trusted local reporting and image provenance before judging.
source_note: Educational simulation about image verification and provenance.

title: Crime Chart Without Baseline
post_text: Crime is up 400%! This tiny chart proves nobody is safe anymore.
red_flags: No baseline; unclear timeframe; fear wording; unlabeled chart
explanation: A percentage can sound dramatic when the baseline is small. Statistical claims need context, definitions, and source data.
source_note: Educational simulation based on common misleading graph tactics.

title: Guaranteed Scholarship Fee
post_text: Congratulations! Pay a $39 processing fee today and we guarantee a $10,000 scholarship. No essay, no transcript.
red_flags: Upfront fee; guaranteed award; pressure; no eligibility details
explanation: Scholarship scams often promise guaranteed money in exchange for fees or personal data. Real scholarships publish eligibility rules and official contact information.
source_note: Educational simulation based on scholarship scam prevention guidance.

title: Share If You Care
post_text: If you don't repost this warning in 10 minutes, you clearly don't care about children.
red_flags: Guilt pressure; no evidence; forced sharing
explanation: The post attempts to manipulate identity and emotion rather than provide evidence. Responsible users pause before amplifying pressure-based posts.
source_note: Educational simulation based on emotional manipulation patterns.

title: Celebrity Quote Screenshot
post_text: Famous actor says students should stop reading books. Screenshot proves it.
red_flags: Screenshot only; no interview link; sensational quote
explanation: Screenshots are easy to fabricate. Verify quotes through original interviews, official accounts, or reputable entertainment/news outlets.
source_note: Educational simulation based on fake quote patterns.

title: Public Health Department Update
post_text: Flu clinic hours are extended this weekend. Details and appointment links are posted on countyhealth.gov/flu.
red_flags: Official account; specific details; official domain
explanation: This post provides a specific public-service update from an official source and points to an official domain for details.
source_note: Educational simulation of a trustworthy official update.

title: Deepfake Endorsement
post_text: A famous scientist just endorsed our coin in this shocking video. Buy before midnight.
red_flags: Financial pressure; celebrity/scientist endorsement; shocking video; deadline
explanation: AI-generated or manipulated videos can impersonate trusted people. Combined with investment pressure, this is high-risk and should be reported.
source_note: Educational simulation about deepfakes and investment scams.

title: Study Headline Overreach
post_text: New study proves eating one berry prevents all cancer. Big hospitals are silent.
red_flags: Overstates study; absolute wording; conspiracy claim
explanation: A single study rarely proves an absolute medical outcome. Look for study design, peer review, expert consensus, and cautious wording.
source_note: Educational simulation based on health headline exaggeration.

title: School Closure Rumor
post_text: My cousin heard all schools are closed tomorrow because of a threat. Everyone stay home.
red_flags: Secondhand source; safety claim; no official notice
explanation: Safety announcements should be verified through official school or local authority channels before being shared.

title: Invoice Attachment Message
post_text: Invoice overdue. Open the attached file and enable macros to avoid legal action.
red_flags: Unexpected attachment; macro request; legal threat
explanation: Unexpected attachments and macro requests are classic malware risks. Verify invoices through known contacts and report suspicious messages.
source_note: Educational simulation based on malware phishing patterns.

title: Recycled Disaster Image
post_text: This heartbreaking photo is from the earthquake happening right now. Donate through my wallet link.
red_flags: Donation wallet; no charity identity; image context unknown
explanation: Disaster images are often recycled or miscaptioned. Verify image date/location and donate through established organizations.

title: Survey Sample Trick
post_text: 92% of people want this law passed, according to our poll of 25 followers.
red_flags: Tiny sample; self-selected audience; broad conclusion
explanation: A small self-selected poll cannot represent the public. Sample size, sampling method, and population matter.

title: Library Media Literacy Workshop
post_text: Join our free media literacy workshop Saturday at 2 p.m. Registration is available on the official library website.
red_flags: Official organization; low-risk event; verifiable website
explanation: The post is specific, low pressure, and points to an official website where users can confirm details.
source_note: Educational simulation of a credible civic announcement.

title: Fake Verification Badge
post_text: We are verified by the government. Send your Social Security number by DM to reserve your scholarship.
red_flags: Requests SSN by DM; vague government claim; scholarship pressure
explanation: Sensitive identity data should never be sent by direct message. Verify scholarship programs through official institutional sites.
source_note: Educational simulation based on identity theft risks.
